question: [File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate] Can an unprivileged borrower, under preconditions where they have activated MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER (16) collaterals each backed by a maximally gas-consuming IOracle.price() implementation, force a liquidator calling liquidate() to exhaust the block gas limit via the while(_collateralBitmap != 0) loop that calls IOracle(_collateralParam.oracle).price() for every set bit, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS blocking liquidation of undercollateralized positions? Proof idea: deploy 16 oracles each consuming ~100k gas in price(), activate all 16 collaterals on a borrower, make position unhealthy, call liquidate() and measure gas; assert gas > block_gas_limit.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: isHealthy] Can an unprivileged borrower, under preconditions where they have activated 16 collaterals each with a near-gas-limit IOracle.price() view function, cause isHealthy() called at the end of take() (line 476) to revert with out-of-gas, violating the invariant that the seller health check must complete after every take, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where the seller's take always reverts and their position cannot be unwound? Proof idea: fuzz oracle gas consumption per collateral from 1k to 500k, assert take() reverts when total oracle gas across 16 collaterals exceeds remaining gas budget.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: touchMarket] Can an unprivileged market creator, under preconditions where they supply a Market struct with MAX_COLLATERALS (128) collateral entries each with a distinct valid oracle address, cause touchMarket() to consume excessive gas in the for loop (lines 762-773) iterating over all 128 collateralParams, violating the invariant that market creation must be economically feasible, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where market creation transactions are priced out of blocks or fail? Proof idea: deploy a market with 128 collaterals, measure gas for touchMarket(), assert gas cost is within acceptable block limits.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower, under preconditions where they have activated 16 collaterals each with a gas-heavy IOracle.price() call, cause withdrawCollateral() to revert out-of-gas via the isHealthy() call (line 568) which loops over all activated collaterals, violating the invariant that healthy borrowers must be able to withdraw collateral, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where collateral is permanently locked? Proof idea: activate 16 collaterals with expensive oracles, attempt withdrawCollateral() with a small amount, assert revert due to gas exhaustion in isHealthy().

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall] Can an unprivileged taker or lender, under preconditions where they compose a multicall() array with a large number of calls each triggering isHealthy() via take() or withdrawCollateral(), cause the multicall loop (lines 212-219) to consume unbounded gas proportional to calls.length * 16 oracle calls per health check, violating the invariant that multicall must not bypass per-action gas invariants, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where any multicall involving health checks is blocked? Proof idea: fuzz calls.length from 1 to 100, each call being a take() with a 16-collateral seller, measure total gas and assert it scales linearly with oracle gas cost.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate] Can an unprivileged borrower, under preconditions where they activate exactly MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER (16) collaterals and each IOracle.price() call returns after consuming exactly (block_gas_limit / 17) gas, cause the liquidate() while loop to exhaust gas before reaching the repay/seize logic, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: permanent liquidation DoS leaving bad debt unrealized? Proof idea: binary search oracle gas cost threshold where liquidate() fails, assert any oracle consuming above threshold blocks liquidation.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate + isHealthy] Can an unprivileged borrower, under preconditions where they activate 16 collaterals with oracles that consume gas proportional to a storage variable they control, dynamically increase oracle gas cost after borrowing to make liquidation revert, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: attacker-controlled liquidation DoS enabling indefinite undercollateralized borrowing? Proof idea: deploy oracle that reads from a large storage array whose size the borrower controls; grow array after borrow; assert liquidate() reverts.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: touchMarket] Can an unprivileged market creator, under preconditions where they create a market with 128 collaterals all sharing the same loan token but with distinct oracle addresses, cause the for loop in touchMarket() to iterate 128 times storing collateral params, making subsequent calls to take() or liquidate() that re-invoke touchMarket() via the market struct also expensive, violating the invariant that market creation cost must be bounded, causing scoped impact: gas griefing of any user who interacts with a 128-collateral market? Proof idea: measure gas for touchMarket() with N=1,16,64,128 collaterals; assert gas scales linearly and exceeds safe thresholds at N=128.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: isHealthy] Can an unprivileged borrower, under preconditions where they activate 16 collaterals and each oracle's price() function performs a staticcall to another contract that itself loops over storage, cause isHealthy() to consume gas proportional to the depth of oracle call chains, violating the invariant that health checks must complete within a predictable gas budget, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where withdrawCollateral() and take() always revert for this borrower? Proof idea: deploy a chain of oracle contracts each delegating to the next; measure isHealthy() gas as chain depth increases from 1 to 10.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate] Can an unprivileged borrower, under preconditions where they have 16 activated collaterals and the liquidate() while loop (lines 607-618) calls IOracle.price() for each, cause the badDebt computation to be gas-blocked before it completes, leaving _position.debt partially updated and marketState.lossFactor in an inconsistent state if the transaction runs out of gas mid-loop, violating the invariant that bad debt must reduce lender credit exactly once and proportionally, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where lossFactor is not updated but debt is partially reduced? Proof idea: use a test harness that limits gas mid-execution; assert that partial loop execution cannot leave storage in a half-updated state.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: flashLoan] Can an unprivileged caller, under preconditions where they pass a tokens[] array of length N (unbounded by the protocol) to flashLoan(), cause the two for loops (lines 742-744 and 749-751) to iterate N times each performing a safeTransfer/safeTransferFrom, violating the invariant that flashLoan must complete within block gas limits, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where flashLoan with large token arrays blocks the transaction? Proof idea: fuzz tokens.length from 1 to 1000, measure gas, assert gas grows linearly and exceeds block limit at some N.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: take + isHealthy] Can an unprivileged taker, under preconditions where the seller (maker of a sell offer) has 16 activated collaterals each with an expensive oracle, cause the isHealthy() call at line 476 of take() to consume so much gas that the entire take() reverts, violating the invariant that valid offers must be fillable, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where a maker's sell offers become permanently unfillable by any taker? Proof idea: create a sell offer from a maker with 16 expensive-oracle collaterals; attempt take() from any taker; assert revert due to gas exhaustion in isHealthy().

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under preconditions where a borrower has 16 activated collaterals and one oracle at index i returns a price that causes mulDivDown overflow in the maxDebt accumulation (line 613), cause the while loop to revert mid-iteration leaving the liquidation incomplete, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: liquidation failure for positions with extreme oracle prices? Proof idea: fuzz oracle prices near type(uint256).max / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE boundary; assert liquidate() either completes or reverts atomically without partial state.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: isHealthy] Can an unprivileged borrower, under preconditions where they activate 16 collaterals and then one oracle begins reverting (e.g., due to a paused price feed), cause isHealthy() to revert for their position, making withdrawCollateral() permanently revert for that borrower, violating the invariant that healthy accounts must be able to exit, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where collateral is permanently locked due to a single reverting oracle? Proof idea: activate 16 collaterals, pause one oracle, call withdrawCollateral() and assert revert; verify no alternative exit path exists.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: supplyCollateral + collateralBitmap] Can an unprivileged borrower, under preconditions where they supply collateral at index 0 then withdraw it (clearing the bit), then re-supply at index 0 (re-setting the bit), repeatedly cycling to keep collateralBitmap at MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER (16) bits set, cause every subsequent isHealthy() and liquidate() call to iterate the maximum 16 oracle calls, violating the invariant that gas cost of health checks must be bounded by actual active collateral, causing scoped impact: sustained gas griefing of liquidators and health-check callers? Proof idea: fuzz collateral supply/withdraw sequences; assert isHealthy() gas is always proportional to countBits(collateralBitmap).

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall + touchMarket] Can an unprivileged market creator, under preconditions where they compose a multicall() with N calls each invoking touchMarket() with a distinct 128-collateral market, cause the multicall loop to iterate N * 128 collateral validation steps, violating the invariant that multicall must not bypass per-action gas invariants, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where multicall-based market creation is prohibitively expensive? Proof idea: measure gas for multicall([touchMarket(128-collateral-market)] * N) for N in [1,5,10]; assert gas scales as O(N * 128).

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate] Can an unprivileged borrower, under preconditions where they activate 16 collaterals and the liquidate() while loop calls msb(_collateralBitmap) then clearBit() on each iteration, cause the loop to execute exactly 16 iterations each with an external IOracle.price() call, and if the 16th oracle call is designed to consume exactly enough gas to prevent the subsequent repay/seize logic from executing, cause a DoS on the final liquidation step, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: liquidation DoS at the last collateral? Proof idea: set oracle gas cost for the last collateral (highest index) to consume remaining gas; assert liquidate() reverts after processing 15 collaterals.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate + badDebt] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under preconditions where a borrower has 16 activated collaterals and the badDebt computation in liquidate() (lines 614-616) uses zeroFloorSub with mulDivUp for each collateral, cause the accumulated gas cost of 16 mulDivUp calls plus 16 IOracle.price() external calls to push the transaction near the gas limit, violating the invariant that bad debt realization must be economically viable for liquidators, causing scoped impact: bad debt permanently unrealized due to gas cost exceeding liquidator profit? Proof idea: measure liquidate() gas with 16 collaterals at various oracle gas costs; assert there exists a configuration where gas cost exceeds liquidation profit.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: touchMarket] Can an unprivileged market creator, under preconditions where they create a market with 128 collaterals where each collateral token address is just above the previous (sorted ascending), cause the for loop in touchMarket() to perform 128 address comparisons and 128 isLltvAllowed() checks and 128 maxLif() computations, making the total gas cost of market creation exceed a safe threshold, violating the invariant that permissionless market creation must be economically feasible, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where legitimate market creation is blocked by high gas costs? Proof idea: benchmark touchMarket() gas with N=1,32,64,128 collaterals; assert gas at N=128 is within 2x block gas limit.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: isHealthy + take] Can an unprivileged taker, under preconditions where they are the buyer and the seller (maker of a sell offer) has 16 collaterals with oracles that each consume gas proportional to a Merkle proof verification, cause the isHealthy() call at the end of take() to consume O(16 * proof_depth) gas, violating the invariant that take() must complete within a predictable gas budget, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where any take against a maker with complex oracles always reverts? Proof idea: deploy oracle that verifies a Merkle proof of depth D in price(); measure take() gas as D increases from 1 to 20.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate] Can an unprivileged borrower, under preconditions where they have 16 activated collaterals and the liquidate() function is called with postMaturityMode=false, cause the while loop to compute maxDebt across all 16 collaterals before the NotLiquidatable check (line 620), meaning even if the position is healthy the full oracle loop runs, violating the invariant that gas cost must be proportional to actual liquidation work, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where liquidators waste gas on healthy positions with expensive oracles? Proof idea: call liquidate() on a healthy position with 16 expensive oracles; measure gas wasted before NotLiquidatable revert.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall + liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under preconditions where they compose a multicall() with M liquidate() calls each targeting a different borrower with 16 collaterals, cause the multicall loop to execute M * 16 oracle calls, violating the invariant that multicall must not amplify gas costs beyond block limits, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where batch liquidation via multicall is infeasible? Proof idea: measure multicall([liquidate(16-collateral-borrower)] * M) gas for M in [1,5,10]; assert gas scales as O(M * 16 * oracle_gas).

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: isHealthy] Can an unprivileged borrower, under preconditions where they activate 16 collaterals and each oracle's price() function performs a SLOAD from a cold storage slot (2100 gas each), cause isHealthy() to consume at minimum 16 * 2100 = 33,600 gas just for oracle storage reads, plus external call overhead (~2600 gas each), totaling ~75,200 gas for oracle calls alone, violating the invariant that health checks must be gas-efficient, causing scoped impact: gas griefing making health checks expensive for all callers? Proof idea: deploy 16 oracles each reading from cold storage; measure isHealthy() gas; assert it exceeds a reasonable health-check gas budget.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate + collateralBitmap] Can an unprivileged borrower, under preconditions where they activate collaterals at indices 0 and 127 (highest and lowest bits of the 128-bit bitmap), cause the while loop in liquidate() to call msb() returning 127 first, then clearBit(127), then msb() returning 0, executing exactly 2 oracle calls but with the msb() computation on a sparse bitmap, and verify that the loop terminates correctly without skipping any activated collateral, violating the invariant that multi-collateral valuation must not skip or double count collateral, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption in maxDebt or badDebt? Proof idea: activate collaterals at indices 0 and 127; assert liquidate() calls price() exactly twice and computes maxDebt correctly.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: touchMarket + collateralParams] Can an unprivileged market creator, under preconditions where they create a market with 128 collaterals where collateral at index 127 has an oracle that will later revert, cause any borrower who activates that collateral to have their isHealthy(), withdrawCollateral(), and take() (as seller) permanently revert, violating the invariant that exit paths must remain available, causing scoped impact: fund freeze for any borrower who activates the reverting-oracle collateral? Proof idea: create 128-collateral market; borrower activates collateral[127]; pause oracle[127]; assert withdrawCollateral() reverts for that borrower.
